---
title: "The pipeline"
description: "Tiered replication, the estimand router, the causal-ML extension, and the blinding rule."
---

## At a glance

Each box below is an **isolated agent** that communicates only through files. The
**replication gate** halts the run before any ML if the replication fails; the
**AI-review loop** iterates up to two rounds, and the DML-technical referee is the
sole authority to block.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    IN([Paper PDF + replication package])
    S1["Stage 1 · Paper intelligence<br/>read code first → paper_spec.json"]
    S2["Stage 2 · Data prep<br/>→ dataset.parquet"]
    S3["Stage 3 · Replication<br/>tiered verdict per coefficient"]
    GATE{Replication gate}
    HALT([Halt — no ML, no fabricated numbers])
    S4["Stage 4 · Causal-ML extension<br/>estimand router · DoubleML · benchmark-blinded"]
    S5["Stage 5 · Diagnostics"]
    S6["Stage 6 · Report<br/>gap table + estimand statement"]
    OUT([Honest report + website page])

    subgraph ROUTER["estimand router — picks ONE, on design × treatment"]
      direction LR
      M1[PLR]
      M2[IRM + generic ML]
      M3[PLIV / IIVM]
      M4[DID / DID_PLR]
    end

    subgraph REVIEW["AI peer review — up to 2 rounds"]
      RG[General referee]
      RT["DML-technical referee<br/>19-point audit · sole authority to block"]
      SY[Synthesis QC]
      DEC{Blocking issue?}
      RVA[Revision agent]
      FIN[Final referee]
      RG --> SY
      RT --> SY
      SY --> DEC
      DEC -- yes --> RVA
      RVA -. next round .-> RG
      RVA -. next round .-> RT
      DEC -- no --> FIN
    end

    IN --> S1 --> S2 --> S3 --> GATE
    GATE -- "FAIL: sign flip / sig change" --> HALT
    GATE -- "PASS / documented partial" --> S4
    S4 --> ROUTER --> S5 --> S6
    S6 --> RG
    S6 --> RT
    FIN --> OUT

    class IN,OUT io
    class S1,S2,S3,S4,S5,S6 ag
    class M1,M2,M3,M4 mod
    class RG,RT,SY,DEC,RVA,FIN ref
    class HALT stop
    classDef ag fill:#eef3f8,stroke:#2c5f8a,stroke-width:1px,color:#16242f;
    classDef mod fill:#ffffff,stroke:#2c5f8a,stroke-dasharray:4 2,color:#16242f;
    classDef ref fill:#f4f6f8,stroke:#6c757d,color:#16242f;
    classDef io fill:#2c5f8a,stroke:#2c5f8a,color:#ffffff;
    classDef stop fill:#f8d7da,stroke:#b5462f,color:#7a1f12;
```

## Step 1 — Replicate (tiered)

RECAST first reproduces the paper's headline result *coefficient by coefficient*,
at the paper's own standard-error convention (classical, robust, or clustered —
getting this wrong is a common silent error). Two regimes:

- **Deterministic** — the original code/data are on disk, so each headline
  coefficient is graded against the printed table:
  - **Success** — coefficient and t/SE match to printed precision, n exact.
  - **Partial** — sign and significance preserved, n within tolerance, *and* every
    numeric gap has a specific documented cause.
  - **Failure** — a sign flip, a significance-category change, or an unexplained gap.
- **Stochastic** — no original package is available, so there is no deterministic
  target; RECAST records a documented *partial* and defers the comparison to the
  benchmark gap table (this is how the teacher-training paper is handled, honestly).

**The failure gate is enforced in code.** If replication fails, the pipeline
**stops before any ML runs** — no extension, no fabricated numbers. A deliberate
fault-injection test (a sign-flipped target) confirms this: stage 4 halts in
seconds and writes a `failed` status rather than producing an estimate.

## Step 2 — Extend (causal ML)

The estimand is chosen by a **total routing rule** on design × treatment type, not
a default:

| Design × treatment | Model | Heterogeneity |
|---|---|---|
| RCT, binary | IRM (ATE / ATTE) | generic ML (BLP/GATES/CLAN) — *only* here |
| RCT / selection-on-observables, continuous | PLR | PLR `gate()`/`cate()` (exploratory) |
| selection-on-observables, binary | IRM + trimming | IRM `gate()`/`cate()` + causal forest |
| IV, binary Z & D | IIVM (LATE) | — |
| IV, continuous | PLIV | — |
| multivalued | APOS / pairwise IRM | — |
| panel / DiD, continuous intensity | PLR on two-way-FE panel (`DID_PLR`) | event-study aggregation |
| panel / DiD, binary staggered | `DoubleMLDIDMulti` (→ `DID_PLR` fallback if N is small) | group-time ATTs |
| anything unmatched | **flag for human** | — |

The conditioning set is **inherited variable-for-variable** from the original — no
data-driven control selection (that would invite bad-control and bias-amplification
problems). Learners may shrink a control's contribution; the agent may not drop it.
Within that fixed set, nuisances are flexible: pairwise interactions for penalized
learners, raw controls for trees. Seven learners, median aggregation across 100
sample splits, and the Baiardi–Naghi adjusted standard error.

## Step 3 — The blinding rule

In benchmark mode the extension agent **never sees the benchmark numbers**. The
orchestrator computes the gap table only *after* `dml_results.json` is written and
SHA-256-frozen — so "consistency" with a published benchmark can never be the
result of tuning toward it. Tuning decisions must be justified by out-of-fold
nuisance RMSE, logged before the gap table exists.

## Step 4 — Report

Each run produces a **gap table** (original | ours | benchmark | difference, with a
two-layer materiality verdict), an **estimand statement** (estimand, population,
inherited assumptions, and the correct interpretation under heterogeneity), and a
plain-English verdict. Everything is append-only and auditable.

[See the two-referee review →](review.qmd) · [What has been tested →](coverage.qmd)
